name: PR #3 Review Remediation (post-phase amendment)

files_modify.purpose: Four hardenings, all cited by run_state.json history event 2026-07-08T22:40:00Z (amendment_authorized) as still-valid PR #3 findings; triggers, job shape, `fetch-depth: 0`, `pip install jsonschema pyyaml`, and the `run: bash tools/validate.sh` step are UNCHANGED. (a) Pin both `uses:` refs to source-verified commit SHAs with trailing version comments, replacing floating major-version tags: `uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2` and `uses: actions/setup-python@0b93645e9fea7318ecaed2b359559ac225c90a2b # v5.3.0` -- both 40-char SHAs were verified against the GitHub API by the orchestrator and recorded as source-verified in the same 2026-07-08T22:40:00Z history event; this contract consumes that provenance, it does not re-verify the SHAs itself. (b) Add a top-level `permissions:` block scoped to least privilege: `contents: read`. (c) Add a top-level `concurrency:` block: `group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}` and `cancel-in-progress: true`, so overlapping runs on the same ref are superseded rather than queued. (d) Add `persist-credentials: false` to the existing `actions/checkout` step's `with:` block, alongside the existing `fetch-depth: 0` key (both keys coexist under the same `with:` mapping).

Two fixes, both cited by the same 2026-07-08T22:40:00Z history event. (a) check_c5_branding (~lines 474-494): the per-target existence loop currently calls `die "C5 leakage sweep target '${f}' is missing..."` on a missing file, and `die()` unconditionally exits the entire script (see lines 33-36), which aborts before C6-C8 run and before the `SUMMARY:` line prints -- inconsistent with every other check_* function (e.g. check_c6_bootstrap, lines 500-505), which report failure locally via `echo "[CN] FAIL ..."` + an explanatory `echo "  ..."` line and `return 1`, letting main()'s `&&/||` accumulation continue to the next check. Replace the `die` call: collect missing targets into an array during the existence loop instead of dying inline, and if any are missing, `echo "[C5] FAIL branding-leakage"`, echo an explanatory line naming the missing target(s) (preserving the existing refusing-to-trust-an-absence-of-match rationale), and `return 1` -- skipping the grep sweep for that invocation, exactly as check_c6_bootstrap skips its `bash -n` step when `./bootstrap.sh` is absent. The grep-hit failure path and the PASS path (lines 486-493) are unchanged. (b) frontmatter_python (~lines 219-226): the single-leading-HTML-comment tolerance currently applies to every path passed to the helper, keyed only on the first line's shape, not on where the file lives. `path` (sys.argv[2]) is already passed into the same Python heredoc that reads the tolerance condition, so no new argument threading is required -- add a `path.startswith("tools/fixtures/")` guard to the existing `if lines and lines[0].strip().startswith("<!--")...` condition so the tolerance activates ONLY for paths under `tools/fixtures/`. Update the adjacent comment (currently describing the tolerance as a repo-wide no-op for shipped docs) to instead state the now-true, narrower behavior: the tolerance is fixture-scoped, and any shipped (non-fixtures) `.md` with a leading HTML comment before its frontmatter now fails C1 with the existing 'does not begin with a `---` frontmatter delimiter' message. No other frontmatter_python logic (YAML parse, schema/format branches) changes.

Two changes. (a) SS3's layout tree (lines 53-57) gains a `tools/fixtures/` line consistent with the tree's existing style (box-drawing prefix + right-aligned `#` comment), documenting the negative fixtures already shipped under F-012: since `tools/validate.sh` is no longer the last entry once `tools/fixtures/` is added, its prefix changes from `└──` to `├──` and the new `tools/fixtures/` line becomes the final `└──` entry, e.g. `└── tools/fixtures/         # Negative fixtures for tools/validate.sh --target (F-012)`. (b) Frontmatter (lines 9-16): bump `version` 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1 and `spec_version` "1.0.0" -> "1.0.1" (PATCH -- editorial/documentation addition to an already-implemented feature's layout record, not a new capability), and append one `change_log` entry (NDS SS4) dated after the existing 1.0.0 entry describing the `tools/fixtures/` tree-line addition per feature 016.

non_goals: No changes to the workflow's triggers (pull_request / push-to-main), job/step shape, fetch-depth: 0, pip install line, or the `run: bash tools/validate.sh` step itself

No validator check-logic changes beyond check_c5_branding's missing-target handling and frontmatter_python's comment-tolerance scoping -- C1-C4, C6-C8, and the rest of C5 (the grep sweep and its extra-targets list) are unchanged
No edits to tools/fixtures/*.md or tools/fixtures/broken_index.json (the existing negative fixtures) -- feature 016 only documents their directory in the spec tree, it does not modify them

No edits to any immutable completed-feature artifact: .agent/contracts/000.json-015.json, .agent/qa/*.json, .agent/evidence/000-*.txt through 015-*.txt, or feature_list_002.json's acceptance-test text for any already-complete feature
No CHANGELOG.md edit -- this amendment is not a phase-close or release event
No manifest.json edit -- the manifest stale-note fix is an orchestrator state correction handled outside this contract, per the 2026-07-08T22:40:00Z history event
No .agent/run_state.json edit by the generator -- run_state is orchestrator-owned (AGF Sec 5 rule 4; see the 2026-07-08T16:30:00Z state_correction history precedent)
No re-verification of the two commit SHAs against the GitHub API -- this contract consumes the orchestrator's already-recorded source-verified provenance, it does not re-derive it
No edit to any file beyond .github/workflows/compliance.yml, tools/validate.sh, .agent/product_spec_002.md, and this contract file

verification.acceptance_test: compliance.yml pins both actions to full 40-char commit SHAs with version comments: grep -Ec 'uses: actions/(checkout|setup-python)@[0-9a-f]{40} # v' .github/workflows/compliance.yml outputs 2
verification.command: f=.github/workflows/compliance.yml; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; cnt=$(grep -Ec 'uses: actions/(checkout|setup-python)@[0-9a-f]{40} # v' "$f"); echo "count=$cnt"; test "$cnt" -eq 2 && echo PASS || { echo "expected 2 SHA-pinned uses: lines, got $cnt"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: compliance.yml declares permissions contents: read, a concurrency group with cancel-in-progress true, and persist-credentials: false (python3 yaml structural assertions)
verification.command:
f=.github/workflows/compliance.yml; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import yaml
doc = yaml.safe_load(open('$f'))
assert doc.get('permissions') == {'contents': 'read'}, f\"permissions mismatch: {doc.get('permissions')}\"
conc = doc.get('concurrency')
assert isinstance(conc, dict), f\"concurrency missing or not a mapping: {conc}\"
assert 'group' in conc and conc['group'], f\"concurrency.group missing/empty: {conc}\"
assert conc.get('cancel-in-progress') is True, f\"cancel-in-progress not True: {conc.get('cancel-in-progress')}\"
steps = doc['jobs']['validate']['steps']
checkout_steps = [s for s in steps if str(s.get('uses', '')).startswith('actions/checkout@')]
assert checkout_steps, 'no actions/checkout step found'
with_block = checkout_steps[0].get('with', {})
assert with_block.get('persist-credentials') is False, f\"persist-credentials not False: {with_block.get('persist-credentials')}\"
assert with_block.get('fetch-depth') == 0, f\"fetch-depth: 0 regressed: {with_block.get('fetch-depth')}\"
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: validate.sh C5 missing-target failure emits [C5] FAIL and still reaches SUMMARY (mutated-copy behavioral test: delete NIZAM.json in a cp -a scratch copy, run validator, assert non-zero exit AND '[C5] FAIL' AND a 'SUMMARY:' line both present)
verification.command: scratch=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"' EXIT; cp -a . "$scratch/repo"; rm -f "$scratch/repo/NIZAM.json"; out=$(cd "$scratch/repo" && bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "rc=$rc"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected non-zero exit with NIZAM.json missing"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C5\] FAIL' || { echo "missing '[C5] FAIL' in output"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Eq '^SUMMARY:' || { echo "missing SUMMARY line -- script aborted before reaching it (die regression)"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: validate.sh frontmatter comment tolerance is fixture-scoped (behavioral: a scratch shipped .md with a leading HTML comment before frontmatter now FAILS C1; the shipped fixtures still parse via --target)
verification.command: scratch=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"' EXIT; cp -a . "$scratch/repo"; cd "$scratch/repo"; mkdir -p docs/architecture; { echo '<!-- not a fixture -->'; sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/p' CONTEXT.md; } > docs/architecture/SCRATCH-COMMENT-TEST.md; out1=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target docs/architecture/SCRATCH-COMMENT-TEST.md 2>&1); rc1=$?; echo "$out1"; test "$rc1" -ne 0 || { echo "expected non-fixture leading-comment doc to FAIL C1"; exit 1; }; echo "$out1" | grep -q "does not begin with a '---' frontmatter delimiter" || { echo "expected the specific C1 delimiter-missing error, got different failure"; exit 1; }; for fx in tools/fixtures/bad_authoritative_source.md tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md; do out2=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$fx" 2>&1); echo "$out2"; echo "$out2" | grep -q "does not begin with a '---' frontmatter delimiter" && { echo "regression: $fx no longer tolerated as a fixture"; exit 1; }; echo "$out2" | grep -q '\[C1\] PASS' || { echo "$fx: C1 did not pass"; exit 1; }; done; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: product_spec_002.md Sec 3 tree includes tools/fixtures/ and its frontmatter carries version 1.0.1 with a matching change_log entry (YAML-parsed)
verification.command:
f=.agent/product_spec_002.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'tools/fixtures/' "$f" || { echo "Sec 3 tree missing tools/fixtures/ line"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
lines = open('$f', encoding='utf-8').read().splitlines()
assert lines[0].strip() == '---', 'frontmatter must open with ---'
end = next(i for i in range(1, len(lines)) if lines[i].strip() == '---')
import yaml
fm = yaml.safe_load('\n'.join(lines[1:end]))
assert fm['version'] == '1.0.1', f\"version not 1.0.1: {fm['version']}\"
assert fm['spec_version'] == '1.0.1', f\"spec_version not 1.0.1: {fm['spec_version']}\"
versions = [str(c['version']) for c in fm['change_log']]
assert '1.0.1' in versions, f\"change_log missing 1.0.1 entry: {versions}\"
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: bash tools/validate.sh on the clean tree still prints SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed and exits 0
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected exact summary line 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (not in feature_list_002.json; a scope guard per methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md Sec 5, dogfooded on this contract, matching the pattern used in contracts 014/015): confirms the non-.agent working-tree diff touches exactly compliance.yml + validate.sh, and that the only .agent content change beyond this contract file, this feature's own evidence directory, and the standing run_state.json orchestrator-overhead exclusion (established convention, contracts 008/009 -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and legitimately dirty at evaluator time) is product_spec_002.md.
verification.command: changed_non_agent=$(git diff --name-only HEAD -- . ':(exclude).agent' | LC_ALL=C sort); expected_non_agent=$(printf '%s\n' .github/workflows/compliance.yml tools/validate.sh | LC_ALL=C sort); if [ "$changed_non_agent" != "$expected_non_agent" ]; then echo "scope drift outside .agent -- got:"; echo "$changed_non_agent"; echo "expected:"; echo "$expected_non_agent"; exit 1; fi; changed_agent=$(git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent | LC_ALL=C sort); unexpected_agent=$(echo "$changed_agent" | grep -v -E '^\.agent/product_spec_002\.md$|^\.agent/contracts/016\.json$|^\.agent/evidence/016-verify-[0-9]+\.txt$|^\.agent/run_state\.json$' || true); if [ -n "$unexpected_agent" ]; then echo "unexpected .agent changes:"; echo "$unexpected_agent"; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (equivalence guard): the workflow's extracted run command is still exactly `bash tools/validate.sh` -- catches an accidental rewrite of the invocation line while hardening the surrounding YAML.
verification.command:
f=.github/workflows/compliance.yml; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import yaml
doc = yaml.safe_load(open('$f'))
steps = doc['jobs']['validate']['steps']
run_steps = [s for s in steps if 'run' in s]
matches = [s['run'].strip() for s in run_steps if s['run'].strip() == 'bash tools/validate.sh']
assert matches, f\"no step with run == exactly 'bash tools/validate.sh' -- run steps were: {[s['run'] for s in run_steps]}\"
print('PASS')
"

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/016-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/016-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/016-verify-08.txt for the eighth). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths (mktemp-created scratch directories used by verify-03 and verify-04 are runtime-generated, not session-absolute, and are cleaned up via trap; the recorded invocation itself contains no /tmp session path literal). One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
amendments: 2026-07-08: verification[5] unquoted 1.0.1 literal quoted (SyntaxError -- test could never pass); verification[7] .agent guard gains the standing run_state.json orchestrator-overhead exclusion. Both defects surfaced at implementation; deliverables unaffected.
